title: Configure TLS with safe defaults
description: Set protocol versions, cipher suites, and certificate validation so transport security is not silently downgraded.
body: Steps: 1. Require TLS 1.2+ (prefer 1.3) and disable SSLv3/early TLS. 2. Restrict to AEAD cipher suites with forward secrecy (ECDHE). 3. Enable full certificate-chain and hostname verification; never disable it to silence an error. 4. Manage certificates and renewal (e.g. ACME) before expiry. 5. Test with a TLS scanner and verify no downgrade or weak-suite negotiation. Rollback point: before changing the accepted protocol/suite set in production. Validation: a TLS scan shows only strong protocols/suites and verification rejects an invalid certificate; disabling certificate verification to make a client 'work' is a critical defect, not a fix.
